selection.selectedReason: Concept 002 has the clearest dark left-side title zone, a readable start-button area, no fake UI or title text, and a metallic facility style that matches the current FPS foundation.

styleDecision.font: Wide condensed sci-fi sans, all caps, squared cuts, no decorative rune font for core UI.
styleDecision.colorScheme: Gunmetal and charcoal base, cyan-teal emissive accents, restrained toxic green portal glow, small amber warning highlights, near-white title text with dark shadow.

concepts.prompt: No text, no letters, no logos, no watermark. Create a mobile landscape game title background for a stylized Three.js first-person shooter called Sigilbreaker. A steel corridor opens into a fractured neon-green sigil gate, with low-poly industrial metal walls, tiled floor, faint enemy silhouettes far away, and room for a crisp HTML title in the upper-left third. Keep it readable on a small phone screen, high contrast, not too busy.

No text, no letters, no logos, no watermark. Create a mobile landscape game title background for Sigilbreaker: a ritual-tech foundry hall with a shattered metallic glyph machine embedded in the far wall, steel catwalk pieces, gridded concrete floor, and a small first-person weapon silhouette at bottom-right. Leave a clean title zone along the left side and a start-button zone under it. The image should feel like a playable FPS level, not a marketing poster.

No text, no letters, no logos, no watermark. Create a mobile landscape FPS title background for Sigilbreaker set inside a monster-infested steel bunker room. The room has tall metallic walls, a roof grid, floor tiles, glowing green sigil cracks on one wall, and abstract small creature shapes held back by light. Leave central and upper-left negative space for live HTML title and a start button. It should match a mobile arcade shooter that uses external 3D assets.

risks: Use object-fit-style cover testing across phone landscape widths because edge cropping is expected.
Do not place title or start UI over the circular portal detail on the center-right.
Keep the runtime image compressed; avoid stacking additional full-screen animated assets during boot.
